python_local.code:
import sys

def main():
    text = sys.argv[1]
    n = int(sys.argv[2])
    lines = text.split('\n')
    last_n_lines = '\n'.join(lines[-n:]) + '\n'
    print(last_n_lines)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

sigil.code:
(set lines array (split $0 "\n"))

   ; Count occurrences of each line
   (set counts map {})
   (for-each line string lines
     (if (not (map_has counts line))
       (set counts (map_set counts line 0)))
     (set counts (map_set counts line (add 1 (map_get counts line)))))

   ; Convert to array of [line, count] pairs for sorting
   (set pairs array [])
   (for-each key string (map_keys counts)
     (push pairs [key (map_get counts key)]))

   ; Sort by count descending, then first occurrence
   (set sorted_pairs array (sort_by pairs (\pair (- (last pair) (* 1000000 (first (enumerate lines)))))))

   ; Print each distinct line and its count
   (for-each pair array sorted_pairs
     (println (fmt "   {} {}" (last pair) (first pair))))
(println (count $0 "\n"))

python_local.code:
import sys

def filter_filenames(filenames, extension):
    return '\n'.join([filename for filename in filenames.split('\n') if filename.endswith('.' + extension)]) + '\n'

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_data = sys.argv[1]
    target_extension = sys.argv[2]
    result = filter_filenames(input_data, target_extension)
    print(result)

python_local.code:
import sys

def filter_csv_rows(csv_string, column_index, threshold):
    rows = csv_string.strip().split('\n')
    filtered_rows = [row for row in rows if int(row.split(',')[int(column_index)]) > int(threshold)]
    return '\n'.join(filtered_rows)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    csv_input = sys.argv[1]
    column_index = sys.argv[2]
    threshold = sys.argv[3]
    result = filter_csv_rows(csv_input, column_index, threshold)
    print(result)

    print("Usage: python script.py <text> <substring>")

python_local.code:
import sys

lines = sys.argv[1].split('\n')
seen = set()
result = []

for line in lines:
    if line not in seen:
        result.append(line)
        seen.add(line)

print('\n'.join(result))

    print("Usage: python script.py <text> <target_string> <replacement_string>")
else:
    text = sys.argv[1]
    target_string = sys.argv[2]
    replacement_string = sys.argv[3]
    result = text.replace(target_string, replacement_string)
    print(result + '\n')

sigil.code:
(set sizes array ["B" "KB" "MB" "GB" "TB"])
   (set divisors array [1 1024 1024*1024 1024*1024*1024 1024*1024*1024*1024])

   ; Get the byte count from CLI argument and convert it to float
   (set bytes int #0)
   (set bytes_float float (cast_int_float bytes))

   ; Find the appropriate size unit
   (set i int 0)
   (while (and (lt i 4) (ge bytes_float (array_get divisors (add i 1))))
     (set i (add i 1)))

   ; Calculate the size in the chosen unit and print it with 2 decimal places
   (println (fmt "{:.2f} {}" (div bytes_float (array_get divisors i)) (array_get sizes i)))

python_local.code:
import sys

def filter_lines(lines, pattern):
    return [line for line in lines if pattern in line and 'grep' not in line]

if __name__ == "__main__":
    input_data = sys.argv[1]
    pattern = sys.argv[2]
    lines = input_data.split('\n')
    filtered_lines = filter_lines(lines, pattern)
    print('\n'.join(filtered_lines))

python_local.code:
import sys

input_data = sys.argv[1]
lines = input_data.split('\n')
output = ''

for line in lines:
    if line:
        parts = line.split(' ', 2)
        if len(parts) > 2:
            author = parts[1]
            message = ' '.join(parts[2:])
            output += f'{author}: {message}\n'

print(output.strip())

python_local.code:
import sys

text = sys.argv[1]
lines = text.split('\n')
result = []

i = 0
while i < len(lines):
    count = 1
    while i + 1 < len(lines) and lines[i] == lines[i + 1]:
        i += 1
        count += 1
    result.append(f'{count:4} {lines[i]}')
    i += 1

print('\n'.join(result))

sigil.code:
(set permission-map {
    "0" "---",
    "1" "--x",
    "2" "-w-",
    "3" "-wx",
    "4" "r--",
    "5" "r-x",
    "6" "rw-",
    "7" "rwx"
  })

  (set symbolic "")

  (for-each char (chars $0)
    (set symbolic (string_concat symbolic (map_get permission-map char))))

  (println symbolic)

(set parts (split $0 "."))
   (set is_valid true)
   (if (ne (len parts) 4)
       (set is_valid false))
   (for-each part string parts
     (if (or (eq (len part) 0) (and (gt (len part) 1) (eq (string_at part 0) "0")))
         (set is_valid false))
     (set num (parse_int part))
     (if (or (lt num 0) (gt num 255))
         (set is_valid false)))
   (println (if is_valid "yes" "no"))

; Remove semicolons from comments as they are not allowed in Sigil
   (set text string $0)

   ; Define regex pattern for ISO-format dates
   (set date_pattern string "\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}")  ; Escape backslashes for the regex pattern

   ; Find all matches in the text
   (set dates array (find_all date_pattern text))

   ; Print each match on a new line
   (for-each date string dates
     (println date))

(set tokens (split $0 " "))
   (set counts {})
   (for-each token tokens
     (set counts (map_set counts token (add 1 (get_or counts token 0)))))

   ; Convert map to array of pairs, sort by key
   (set sorted_pairs (sort_by (entries counts) (\pair (first pair))))

   ; Format as "NAME=N" strings and join with spaces
   (set formatted_strings (map_arr sorted_pairs (\pair (fmt "{}={}" (get pair 0) (get pair 1)))))
   (println (join formatted_strings " "))

(set python-source $0)
   (set lines (split python-source "\n"))
   (for-each line string lines
     (if (regex_match "^def \w+\(" line)
       ; Extract name and print
       (println (string_slice line 4 (sub (len line) 5)))))

python_local.code:
import sys

source_code = sys.argv[1]
lines = source_code.split('\n')
function_names = []

for line in lines:
    if line.strip().startswith('def ') and '(' in line:
        function_name = line.split('(')[0].split(' ')[1]
        function_names.append(function_name)

print('\n'.join(function_names))
